results.title: Soil: more than dirt
results.brief_description: This program lokks at land degredation due to salting, soil erosion and soil structure decline because of over use of the land itself, this in turn leads to low agricultural productivity. The research done by the department of Conservation, Forests and Land suggests ways and means of better land management practices. No other credits

results.title: Water erosion and waterlogging
results.creator_credit: Gerry North and Western Australia. Dept. of Agriculture
results.brief_description: Water erosion can result in the complete degradation of land by the removal of topsoils to expose infertile clay subsoils. The adoption of minimum tillage and other farm management techniques can lessen the risk of water erosion and improve farm productivity.

results.title: Future looks rosy
results.brief_description: Looks at the problems of soil salinity due to erosion and poor agricultural practices. In cooperation with the Department of Conservation, Forests & Land, landholders are seeking solutions to the problem of land degradation.

results.title: The weeping earth: salt in South Eastern Australia
results.brief_description: Dr Jim Bowler introduces a program addressing the scourge of the Australian land - salt - a side effect of settlement and clearing. Discusses the situation before European settlement and examines why salt is such a problem by looking at the history of the Australian landscape.

results.title: What's an Accountant?
results.creator_credit: Neil McClure, CPA Australia and Production Group
results.brief_description: Tania Lacey introduces a program designed to encourage viewers to consider the possibility of a career in accounting. Discusses common sterotypes of accountants, the prerequisites for an accountancy career and the diverse range of employment opportunities. Wherever you may wish to work - “Accountancy can take you there!”

results.title: Hidden costs
results.creator_credit: James Mann, Tony Paice and Victoria. Dept. of Education and Early Childhood Development
results.brief_description: Looks at the hidden cost of shoplifting not only to the consumer and shopkeepers, but the cost to the legal system and the consequences for the offender.Aimed at a teenage audience.

results.title: Go Between. My Name Is Asher Lev
results.brief_description: Designed for senior students of English, this programme features a lecture on the “The Go-between”, by L.P. Hartley, and “My name is Asher Lev” by Chaim Potok. Critical issues and significant passages are discussed.

results.title: Occupational health & safety act of Victoria
results.brief_description: A program to explain how the Safety Act relates to the workplace of Victoria. Part 1. Introduction and outline of respective duties of employers manufactures and employees and roles of the Department of Labour. Victorian Occupational Health and Safety Commission and occupational health and safety representatives. Part 2. Looks at keys to success with consultative arrangements for resolving workplace issues. Part 3. Outlines ways problems can be resolved.

results.title: Kids making decisions
results.creator_credit: Tony Paice, Corporate Relations Unit and Victoria. Inner City School Support Centre
results.brief_description: A program for primary teachers highlighting key features in developing integrated curriculum. An integrated curriculum goes beyond a thematic approach and involves coordinating all curriculum areas around one chosen topic to promote the development of learning. Shows teachers and their classes in three Western Metropolitan schools involved in choosing a topic, ordering concept areas and contributing questions. The pupils are involved by taking responsibility for their own learning in making key decisions.
